summary: Large Samus grapple item decomp slice. The PR filled in many src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c functions, added typed Samus grapple item attributes and prototypes, corrected ItemLink-based item vars, and adjusted ftSamus data/motion var typing needed by the grapple logic. decomp.dev reported +3336 matched-code bytes, +16 matched-data bytes, 9 new matches, and 13 near-match improvements; the author explicitly noted many remaining near-matches and duplicated code.

key_files.role: Main Samus grapple implementation work: many former placeholders became C functions and static inlines for link setup, animation, collision, chain movement, state updates, and fighter callbacks.
Changed itSamusGrapple_ItemVars x0/x4 from f32 to ItemLink* and added itSamusGrappleAttributes with floats plus HSD_Joint and animation pointer fields.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes using ItemLink, Vec3, Fighter, bool, s32, and itSamusGrappleAttributes.
Typed ftSs_DatAttrs xA0/xB0 as s32 and xCC as f32, and added an unknown ftSamus_State7Vars motion var containing f32 x0.

what_changed: Implemented or substantially decompiled many Samus grapple symbols in itsamusgrapple.c, including it_802B743C, fn_802B7E34, fn_802B805C, fn_802B8384, fn_802B8524, fn_802B8684, fn_802B8814, fn_802B895C, fn_802B8B54, fn_802B8D38, it_802B900C, it_802B91C4, it_802B9CE8, it_802B9FD4, it_802BA194, it_802BA2D8, it_802BA3BC, it_802BA5DC, it_802BA760, and it_802BACC4.

Added local helpers such as samus_grapple_fighter_compare, samus_grapple_init_link, samus_grapple_calc_grav, samus_grapple_setup_pos, samus_grapple_anim, samus_grapple_state_sync, fn_802B7E34_inline, and fn_802B895C_inline.

Modeled the grapple as an ItemLink chain: x0/x4 in itSamusGrapple_ItemVars are now ItemLink* instead of f32, and existing code was corrected from xDD4_itemVar.linkhookshot.x0 to xDD4_itemVar.samusgrapple.x0 in Samus grapple paths.

Added itSamusGrappleAttributes, covering offsets +00 through +A0 with many f32 parameters, one s32 at +34, HSD_Joint* model assets, and HSD_AnimJoint/HSD_MatAnimJoint/HSD_ShapeAnimJoint pointer tables.

Typed public prototypes in itsamusgrapple.h for collision/check functions and chain update helpers, replacing unknown signatures with ItemLink*, Vec3*, itSamusGrappleAttributes*, Fighter*, f32, bool, and s32 return types.

Implemented HSD object and animation setup in it_802B743C using HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, and HSD_JObjReqAnimAll with null-guarded animation pointer loads.

Implemented tether/chain physics helpers that update positions, velocities, distance constraints, collision data, retraction, floor/wall reflection, and random gravity-like perturbation using ItemLink prev/next traversal.

Integrated Samus grapple item behavior with fighter state through callbacks and checks involving motion_id values, held inputs, ground_or_air, ftCommon, ftCo, ftCliffCommon, ftColl, effects, and fp->fv.ss.x223C.
decomp.dev reported 9 new matches: it_802B9CE8, it_802BACC4, it_802B743C, it_802B900C, it_802B91C4, fn_802B8524, fn_802B8814, it_802BA2D8, and .sdata.

decomp.dev also reported 13 improved but still unmatched items, including fn_802B805C, fn_802B8D38, it_802BA3BC, fn_802B7E34, it_802BA760, fn_802B895C, fn_802B8B54, it_802B9FD4, fn_802B8384, it_802BA5DC, fn_802B8684, it_802BA194, and .sdata2.
smart_moves: The PR first improved structure knowledge: changing Samus grapple item vars to ItemLink* and defining itSamusGrappleAttributes gave the implementation type information needed for meaningful pointer traversal and header prototypes.

It preserved unknown semantic names where evidence was weak: fields stayed as xNN and the new ftSamus_State7Vars member was explicitly marked with a TODO for a proper state name instead of inventing terminology.

Repeated low-level behavior was factored into static inline helpers for motion checks, JObj matrix-to-position setup, animation traversal, input state sync, and collision/path checks, matching the author's note that the file has duplicated code.
The implementation kept HSD/JObj asset setup data-driven through the special attributes table, with separate joint and anim/matanim/shapeanim pointer slots selected by type.
The chain simulation consistently used ItemLink prev/next traversal plus shared distance helpers, making the articulated tether behavior readable even while many symbols remain address-named.
Header signatures were upgraded from unknown prototypes to typed prototypes, which makes later decomp work safer and gives reviewers concrete ABI expectations.
decomp_lessons: Do not assume similar character items share the same itemVar union member: this PR corrected Samus grapple paths from linkhookshot to samusgrapple and typed x0/x4 as ItemLink*.
For character tether items, define the special-attributes struct early; HSD_Joint and animation pointer table offsets are essential for matching constructors and animation setup.

Typed ftSamus dat attributes can be inferred from use sites: this PR changed xA0/xB0 to s32 for comparisons, xCC to f32 for a fighter action call, and added an f32 motion var used by grapple timing logic.

Near-matches in large item systems may require semantic cleanup beyond line-by-line translation; the PR body explicitly says semantic understanding may help organize duplicated code and resolve matches.
Static inline wrappers and explicit local stack padding are useful but can leave near-matches; keep helpers small and verify whether the compiler emits an inline body or a call.
When real names are not known, keep address-based symbols and xNN fields but improve types and prototypes; this still unlocks matches and future readability.
naming_conventions: New semantic type names followed existing item style: itSamusGrapple_ItemVars and itSamusGrappleAttributes.
Unresolved functions retained address-based names such as it_802B900C, it_802BA760, and fn_802B7E34.
Local helper names used lowercase samus_grapple_* for file-private behavior, such as samus_grapple_setup_pos and samus_grapple_state_sync.
Unknown struct fields remained offset-based xNN names, and the unknown Samus motion var stayed unk7 with a TODO for a proper state name.
Existing callback names like itSamusgrapple_UnkMotionN_Phys were preserved rather than renamed without evidence.
itsamusgrapple.h kept address comments next to declarations while upgrading the C types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and explicit u8 _pad arrays around Vec3 and Mtx locals to influence stack layout for matching.

Used static inline helpers for code that appears repeatedly, while fn_802B7E34 still contains an explicit animation block and a commented-out samus_grapple_anim call, suggesting call-vs-inline shape mattered there.
Wrote vector updates field-by-field and sometimes used pointer aliases such as dir_ptr, which is a common tactic for matching register allocation and emitted instruction order.
Kept explicit null checks before dereferencing animation pointer tables in it_802B743C, matching the control-flow shape for anim, matanim, and shapeanim selection.

Separated similar chain-update helpers it_802B900C and it_802B91C4 even though they are duplicated, preserving behavioral/codegen differences such as random gravity adjustment versus attrs->x44 gravity.
Converted unknown prototypes to exact bool/s32/void return types where branch usage made the ABI clear.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body says: lots of near-matches, lots of duplicated code, and semantic understanding would likely help organize the code and resolve matches.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code 59.07% (+0.09%, +3336 bytes) and matched data 37.69% (+16 bytes) for GALE01 from 02eef58 to d2e6663.
Bot feedback showed the work was valuable even though incomplete: 9 new matches and 13 improvements in still-unmatched items.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for semantic names for ftSamus_State7Vars unk7 and ftSs_DatAttrs xA0 xB0 xCC.
Search later PRs for it_802B9328 and it_802B99A0, which remained placeholders/typed prototypes in this slice.
Compare later itsamusgrapple.c changes against this PR to see which near-matches from decomp.dev were resolved.
Look for follow-up cleanup of duplicated chain traversal code in it_802B900C, it_802B91C4, it_802BA194, it_802BA3BC, and it_802BA5DC.
Search ItemLink x2C_b0 x2C_b1 x2C_b2 naming work across hookshot/grapple items.
Check whether later PRs replace raw motion_id constants 0x165, 0x166, 0xD4, and 0xD6 with named FtMotionId constants.
